RESPONSE TO DEFT'S SEP. STATEMENT RE: DEFT'S MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF WALSH, EDWARD AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT EXPLORATORIUM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR AGE DISCRIMINATION. DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF'S REPLACEMENT WAS SIGNIFICANTLY YOUNGER IS NOT A REQUIRED PART OF HIS PRIMA FACIE CASE. FURTHER, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER MR. POWELL (A PERSON IN HIS MID-30'S) REPLACED PLAINTIFF. IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED OTHER EVIDENCE THAT DEMONSTRATES A DISCRIMINATORY MOTIVE. (WALSH DEC., PAR. 12). THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSES OF ACTION (CAUSES OF ACTION 3 AND 4) BECAUSE, FOR THE REASONS SET FORTH ABOVE, DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE AGE DISCRIMINATION. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL DISCHARGE IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HIGHLIGHT A WELL-ESTABLISHED PUBLIC POLICY DELINEATED IN A STATUTE, CONSTITUTION, OR REGULATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENNA WARD, CSR # 7077
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-09-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-01-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-17-2013 Continued To Oct-07-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-29-2013 Continued To Jul-26-13 At 01:00 P.M. In Dept 210

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL FARBSTEIN OF FARBSTEIN & BLACKMAN, TEL. NO. (650) 554-6200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EXPLORATORIUM's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The motion for summary judgment is denied and the motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for age discrimination. Demonstrating that plaintiff's replacement was significantly younger is not a required part of his prima facie case. Further, there is a triable issue of material fact whether Mr. Powell (a person in his mid-30's) replaced plaintiff. In addition, plaintiff has presented other evidence that demonstrates a discriminatory motive. (Walsh dec., par. 12). The motion is denied as to the intentional and negligent infliction of emotional distress causes of action (causes of action 3 and 4) because, for the reasons set forth above, defendant has not established that plaintiff cannot prove age discrimination. The Court grants summary adjudication on the second cause of action for wrongful discharge in violation of public policy. Plaintiff does not highlight a well-established public policy delineated in a statute, constitution, or regulation. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVenna Ward, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-27-2012 Continued To Jan-09-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL FARBSTEIN OF FARBSTEIN & BLACKMAN, TEL. NO. (650) 554-6200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - DEFENDANT EXPLORATORIUM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to January 9, 2013. Pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B, the opposing party shall lodge a courtesy copy of the opposition with department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date by December 10, 2012. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Susan Crozier, CSR # 4761 = (302/MJM)
